description: Data pipeline integrity and degradation preconditions. Conditions whose simultaneous realization constitutes a valid, trustworthy data product. Each failure path corresponds to a class of data quality problems that causes downstream decisions to be made on false certainty.

DP-01.label: schema_contract_present
DP-01.description: A declared schema or contract exists for this stage. Without it the substrate cannot evaluate any other condition — all downstream wickets remain structurally unknown.
DP-01.evidence_hint: Check for schema registry entry, JSON Schema file, dbt model YAML, or CREATE TABLE statement. Evidence rank 3 (config/contract).

DP-02.label: schema_version_stable
DP-02.description: The schema has not changed since the last collection. A schema change without a corresponding migration marks all transformation correctness wickets as unknown.
DP-02.evidence_hint: Compare schema hash or version against last recorded. Evidence rank 3.

DP-03.label: required_fields_populated
DP-03.description: All non-nullable fields declared in the contract have non-null values in this batch or record set. NULL in a required field is a blocked state, not an unknown — the constraint is active.
DP-03.evidence_hint: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE required_field IS NULL. Evidence rank 1 (runtime query).

DP-04.label: fields_within_declared_bounds
DP-04.description: Numeric and date fields fall within the min/max/enum bounds declared in the contract. Out-of-bounds values indicate upstream corruption or schema drift.
DP-04.evidence_hint: SELECT MIN/MAX/COUNT of each bounded field against declared constraints. Evidence rank 1.

DP-05.label: referential_integrity_intact
DP-05.description: Foreign key relationships resolve. No orphaned records. Broken references mean the record cannot be correctly joined downstream — the relationship is blocked, not unknown.
DP-05.evidence_hint: LEFT JOIN target against foreign table; WHERE fk IS NULL in result. Evidence rank 1.

DP-06.label: transformation_applied
DP-06.description: The declared transformation has run on this batch. Detectable via audit log, transformation timestamp column, or comparison of raw vs transformed record counts.
DP-06.evidence_hint: Check transformation audit table or compare row counts pre/post transform. Evidence rank 2 (build/pipeline log).

DP-07.label: transformation_correct
DP-07.description: The transformation output matches the declared contract. A transformation that ran but produced wrong output is a blocked state — the constraint was violated. Different from unknown (not yet run).
DP-07.evidence_hint: Sample transformed values against declared business rules. Unit test assertions on known inputs. Evidence rank 1.

DP-08.label: no_duplicate_records
DP-08.description: The declared primary key appears exactly once per record in this batch. Duplicates introduced by joins, retries, or reprocessing corrupt any aggregate built downstream.
DP-08.evidence_hint: SELECT pk, COUNT(*) GROUP BY pk HAVING COUNT(*) > 1. Evidence rank 1.

DP-09.label: freshness_within_ttl
DP-09.description: The most recent record or batch is within the declared TTL. Stale data feeding a live decision system is a temporal fold — the data was real, but may no longer be.
DP-09.evidence_hint: SELECT MAX(updated_at) and compare to NOW() - declared_ttl. Evidence rank 1.

DP-10.label: upstream_source_reachable
DP-10.description: The upstream data source responds to connection probes. Unreachable upstream is a blocked state for all downstream freshness and completeness wickets.
DP-10.evidence_hint: TCP connect or SELECT 1 against upstream connection string. Evidence rank 4 (network).

DP-11.label: batch_complete
DP-11.description: All expected records for this batch window have arrived. Detected via record count against declared expectation, watermark comparison, or batch manifest check.
DP-11.evidence_hint: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE batch_id = X vs declared_expected_count. Evidence rank 1.

DP-12.label: distribution_stable
DP-12.description: Statistical distribution of key fields is within historical bounds. Drift beyond declared sigma is a contextual fold — the system observed a condition it cannot evaluate against current contracts.
DP-12.evidence_hint: Compare current mean/stddev/percentiles to baseline. Z-score or KL divergence against rolling window. Evidence rank 6 (scanner/profiler).

DP-13.label: no_null_injection
DP-13.description: The transformation did not introduce NULLs into fields that were non-null upstream. NULL injection by transformation is distinct from upstream missing data — it is a transformation defect.
DP-13.evidence_hint: Compare null counts pre/post transformation for each field. Evidence rank 1.

DP-14.label: encoding_consistent
DP-14.description: Character encoding is uniform across the batch and consistent with the declared encoding in the schema contract. Mixed encodings cause silent corruption in string operations.
DP-14.evidence_hint: chardet or Python codecs probe on sample of string fields. Evidence rank 6.

DP-15.label: records_conform_to_current_schema
DP-15.description: Records in this batch conform to the current schema version, not a prior version. After a migration, records written by old producers may violate new constraints silently.
DP-15.evidence_hint: Validate sample records against current JSON Schema or Avro/Protobuf schema. Evidence rank 1.

data_completeness_failure_v1.description: Batch arrived but is incomplete — missing records, null required fields, or broken foreign keys. Any downstream aggregate is wrong.

data_transformation_failure_v1.description: Transformation ran but produced incorrect output — null injection, wrong business rule applied, or output outside declared bounds.

data_drift_undetected_v1.description: Data distribution has shifted beyond declared bounds and freshness has expired. Downstream models or decisions are operating on stale or drifted inputs without knowing it.

schema_migration_breakage_v1.description: Schema changed and old-format records are flowing through the pipeline. Transformation rules written for the new schema silently corrupt or discard old-format fields.

referential_integrity_failure_v1.description: Foreign key relationships broken — orphaned records, dangling references. Any join downstream produces wrong results without error.

stale_feature_vector_v1.description: ML feature vector computed from data that has since changed or expired. Model predictions are based on conditions that no longer reflect reality.

silent_data_corruption_v1.description: Data is present, counts are correct, but values are wrong — encoding errors, null injection, or out-of-bounds values passed validation. No alarm fired.

pipeline_source_loss_v1.description: Upstream source unreachable. All downstream wickets transition to unknown or blocked depending on whether they have cached state.
